title: Miami 7-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Miami 7-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Miami, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Vizcaya Museum and Gardens and Zoo Miami. Prices from $1280 per person (estimate).
highlights: Oceanfront suite at a South Beach luxury hotel
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Miami, United States — Tropical monsoon climate: hot, humid summers (highs 31-33°C, near-daily afternoon thunderstorms) and Atlantic hurricane season June-November, with warm, drier and more comfortable winters (highs 24-26°C). December to April for warm, dry, comfortable weather (highs 24-28°C) and lower hurricane risk; June-November is Atlantic hurricane season with hot, humid, storm-prone conditions. This itinerary blends Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, Zoo Miami, South Beach with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Vizcaya Museum and Gardens, Zoo Miami, South Beach
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Miami
itinerary.description: Land at Miami International Airport (MIA) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Vizcaya Museum and Gardens — Italian Renaissance-style villa and gardens on Biscayne Bay. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Miami: Zoo Miami & Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Zoo Miami — One of the largest tropical zoos in the US. In the afternoon, continue to Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science — Interactive science museum with a planetarium and aquarium. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science

itinerary.title: Miami: Pérez Art Museum Miami (PAMM) & Jungle Island
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Pérez Art Museum Miami (PAMM) — Contemporary art museum on the waterfront. In the afternoon, continue to Jungle Island — Exotic wildlife park and zoo. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Everglades National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Everglades National Park, a popular side trip from Miami. Return to Miami in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Everglades National Park

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Coral Castle, Homestead — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Miami: Everglades airboat tour (Everglades National Park, day trip) & Wynwood Walls
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Everglades airboat tour (Everglades National Park, day trip) — Airboat rides through the sawgrass marshes to spot alligators. In the afternoon, continue to Wynwood Walls — Curated outdoor street-art museum in the Wynwood Arts District. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Everglades airboat tour (Everglades National Park, day trip)

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to South Beach or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MIA for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Everglades airboat tours for the morning — wildlife is more active and it's cooler before midday heat.
December-April is peak season with the best weather and highest hotel prices; book well ahead for Art Basel week in early December.
Parking in South Beach is expensive and limited — consider rideshare instead of a rental car for that specific area.
Cuban coffee (cafecito) at a Little Havana walk-up window is a quintessential, cheap local experience.
Track hurricane forecasts if traveling June-November — the season peaks in August-October.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
A rain jacket for near-daily afternoon summer thunderstorms
A smart-casual outfit for South Beach nightclub dress codes
thingsToDo: Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science
Everglades airboat tour (Everglades National Park, day trip)
Ocean Drive Art Deco Historic District
Airboat tours through Everglades National Park
Jet skiing and parasailing off South Beach
Kayaking through Oleta River State Park mangroves
thingsToAvoid: Renting a car solely for South Beach — parking is scarce and pricey; use it only for out-of-town day trips.
Swimming during Atlantic hurricane season without checking rip current/beach flag warnings.
Skipping travel/medical insurance — US healthcare costs for foreign visitors can be extremely high.
Timeshare-presentation "free gift" pitches on Ocean Drive and near tourist attractions.
Underestimating summer humidity — outdoor sightseeing is best done morning or evening June-September.
topRestaurants: Joe's Stone Crab — Seafood, Miami institution ($$$$)
Yardbird Southern Table & Bar — Southern American ($$$)
instagramSpots: Ocean Drive Art Deco pastel hotels
familyActivities: Phillip and Patricia Frost Museum of Science
adventureActivities: Airboat tours through Everglades National Park
Jet skiing and parasailing off South Beach
Kayaking through Oleta River State Park mangroves
luxuryExperiences: Oceanfront suite at a South Beach luxury hotel
Private yacht charter around Biscayne Bay
VIP table service at LIV or Story nightclub
Fine dining omakase or tasting menu in the Design District
honeymoonExperiences: Private sailing charter at sunset on Biscayne Bay
Couple's spa day at a South Beach resort
Rooftop dinner overlooking the Miami skyline
budgetExperiences: Free South Beach and Miami Beach Boardwalk time
Little Havana Calle Ocho walking tour
Free Bayfront Park events and concerts
localEtiquette: Tipping 18-20% at restaurants is standard and expected, not optional.
South Beach and Art Deco district have relaxed swimwear norms right on the sand, but cover up when entering restaurants and shops.
Spanish is widely spoken, especially in Little Havana — a friendly "gracias" is always appreciated.
Nightclub dress codes are enforced (no flip-flops, athletic wear) at most South Beach venues.
localTransportGuide: Miami is car-dependent overall, but South Beach and downtown are walkable, with the free Metromover downtown people-mover and Uber/Lyft covering the rest. Renting a car is recommended for day trips to the Everglades or Key West.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April for warm, dry, comfortable weather (highs 24-28°C) and lower hurricane risk; June-November is Atlantic hurricane season with hot, humid, storm-prone conditions.
weather: Tropical monsoon climate: hot, humid summers (highs 31-33°C, near-daily afternoon thunderstorms) and Atlantic hurricane season June-November, with warm, drier and more comfortable winters (highs 24-26°C).
visaInformation: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries (including most of Western Europe, UK, Australia, Japan) can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance through a US embassy/consulate interview — a key planning consideration for this site's Indian traveler audience, since processing/interview wait times can run into months.
averageFlightTime: ~15.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Miami?
faqs.answer: Citizens of Visa Waiver Program countries (including most of Western Europe, UK, Australia, Japan) can enter with an approved ESTA for stays up to 90 days. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a B1/B2 visitor visa obtained in advance through a US embassy/consulate interview — a key planning consideration for this site's Indian traveler audience, since processing/interview wait times can run into months.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Miami?
faqs.answer: December to April for warm, dry, comfortable weather (highs 24-28°C) and lower hurricane risk; June-November is Atlantic hurricane season with hot, humid, storm-prone conditions. Tropical monsoon climate: hot, humid summers (highs 31-33°C, near-daily afternoon thunderstorms) and Atlantic hurricane season June-November, with warm, drier and more comfortable winters (highs 24-26°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Miami safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Miami is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Miami?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Noah Patel
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Miami was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Bayside Marketplace was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ava van Dijk
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Wynwood Walls alone was worth the trip, but combined with Pérez Art Museum Miami (PAMM) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Liam Tanaka
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Our trip to Miami was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Zoo Miami was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Patel
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Miami Beach Boardwalk and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
